destinations.evidence: The async pool computes max_poll_block when metadata is created and reads field 4 for the 0x080C sovereign/CLI agent precompile.
The stored field-4 offset is converted to an absolute Phase 2 deadline by adding it to the settlement/commitment block when the pool enters delivery waiting state.

equivalence_argument: The local port names 0x080C as SOVEREIGN_AGENT_PRECOMPILE, but the timeout behavior matches the invariant: field 4 is extracted as max_poll_block and converted into an absolute delivery deadline from the settlement block.
rationale: The sole candidate states the effective timeout rule for CLI_AGENT_PRECOMPILE commitments: decode the max_poll_block offset from input field 4 and add it to commit_block. No in-cluster historical candidate creates a lineage edge.
statement: Async pool timeout calculation for CLI_AGENT_PRECOMPILE commitments must extract max_poll_block from field 4 of the precompile input and convert it to an absolute timeout by adding the offset to commit_block.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a CLI agent async commitment with commit_block 100 and field 4 max_poll_block offset 25, then assert timeout calculation returns 125.
Create comparable commitments for the other two-phase precompile paths and assert their existing offset helpers still determine their deadlines rather than using the CLI agent field 4 helper.
summary: The 0x080C agent timeout is derived from field 4 and converted to an absolute delivery deadline in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create a 0x080C async metadata entry with field 4 set to 25 and a settlement block of 100; max_poll_block should be Some(25) and the delivery deadline should become 125.
title: CLI agent timeout comes from field 4 offset
